description_long: This is a three-story commercial building with a classic, early 20th-century facade, likely constructed from masonry and concrete. The exterior is painted a light beige with ornate window moldings and a decorative cornice along the roofline. Large glass windows dominate the ground floor, suggesting a retail or food service function, while the upper floors feature regularly spaced sash windows. A green downspout runs vertically along the building's corners, and the flat roof includes a small rooftop structure. The sidewalk and street base are textured to simulate an urban environment, and a neon sign reading 'OLLIE' is visible on one side, hinting at the shop's name or brand.
description: A three-story beige masonry pastry shop with ornate window moldings, large ground-floor windows, and a neon 'OLLIE' sign, set on a textured urban base.
description_view_0: Front view with the main entrance, large glass windows, and a neon 'OLLIE' sign on the left side.
description_view_1: Rear view showing the back facade with no signage and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_2: Side view with visible green downspouts and a clear look at the building's depth and window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another green downspout and a partial view of the neon sign's edge.
attribute_storefront_signage: A vertical neon sign with the text 'OLLIE' in blue and red is mounted on the left side of the main facade, above the entrance. The sign is rectangular, with a modern sans-serif font, and is illuminated, making it stand out against the beige exterior. No other signage or logos are visible on the storefront.
attribute_architectural_style: The building exhibits characteristics of late 19th to early 20th-century commercial architecture, with a flat roof, decorative cornice, symmetrical sash windows, and detailed window moldings. The use of masonry, large storefront windows, and ornamental trim suggests a style influenced by Victorian or early Chicago School commercial design.
